note: Step-free entry via the Montague Place north entrance, lifts to all floors, free entry, wheelchairs to borrow. Vast but level throughout.
Historic fortress with cobbles, spiral stairs and the White Tower not step-free; a partial accessible route exists but the Crown Jewels and walls involve stairs. Uneven and tiring.
Fully wheelchair accessible with level boarding onto the capsules (max two wheelchairs per capsule); staff assist. Book a timed slot.
Step-free throughout with lifts to every level, free entry, accessible toilets and viewing level. One of the easiest big attractions.
Level step-free entrance on Trafalgar Square side, lifts between floors, free entry and loaner wheelchairs. Trafalgar Square itself has some steps but ramped routes.

place: Buckingham Palace / Changing of the Guard
note: The forecourt viewing is on level pavement but extremely crowded for the guard change; getting a clear view with a wheelchair or in a stroller is hard. The palace State Rooms (summer) have an accessible route.
Large, flat royal park with paved paths, accessible toilets, the Serpentine and cafes. Ideal for strollers, wheelchairs and seniors.
Wide, flat, step-free promenade along the Thames linking the Eye, Tate and Borough; smooth and pram-friendly with plenty of stops.
Ground-level and mostly step-free but very crowded and tight between stalls; navigating with wheels or a stroller at peak times is a squeeze. Quieter mid-morning.
Level entry and an accessible route through most of the abbey, but some chapels and the Cosmati pavement have steps and the space is dense and busy. Partly accessible.
Step-free access to the cathedral floor and crypt via lift, but the dome galleries are a long stair climb with no step-free option. Main floor fine; dome not accessible.
